headerTitle: Site AI Engineer  ·  Suffolk Construction  ·  Las Vegas, NV  ·  Construction Technology
subjectText: Site AI Engineer — Suffolk Construction (Las Vegas, NV) · $150K–$182K
intro: Suffolk deploys AI on active construction sites to solve the problems contractors actually have: safety monitoring, document Q&A, schedule deviation, subcontractor coordination. I’ve built field operations software for a roofing company from the ground floor — IVR lead intake, crew dispatch, storm-alert campaigns, estimate workflows. I know what field operations look like before an AI layer goes on top of it.
bullets: Construction operations domain — built Roof Works of Texas admin panel end-to-end: estimate wizard, job scheduling, crew dispatch, contract tracking, IVR lead intake, storm-alert campaigns. Roofing is construction — fast-moving field crews, subcontractors, permit timelines, weather risk. I've engineered for this operational environment, not just observed it.

AI deployment into field operations — 14 live AI endpoints wired into real operational workflows: outputs trigger scheduling actions, SMS/IVR campaigns, contract generation, and database state changes. AI that changes what happens in the field, not AI that produces reports.

Production-grade infrastructure — full-stack TypeScript/Next.js 15/Node.js/PostgreSQL on Linux VPS + Docker + PM2 + Nginx across 13 platforms. Site AI Engineer isn't a research role — it's a deployment role. That's the pattern I've run six times.
closingLine: Available immediately. Las Vegas, NV relocation or hybrid considered. US citizen.
matchNotes: On-site Las Vegas construction AI role. Will's RWCR build = direct construction domain experience. AI deployment pattern maps. Main variable: relocation/commute to Las Vegas. Salary $150-182K is strong.
